name: Broiled Beef Tenderloin
recipeIngredient: 2 Beef loin roasts, trimmed to equal size (each about 1 1/2 pounds)
recipeInstructions:
1. About 1 1/2 hours before serving, prepare the mushroom stuffing.
- In a 12-inch skillet over medium heat, melt butter.
- Add mushrooms, ham, onions, salt, and pepper; cook until vegetables are tender, stirring frequently.
- Remove pan from heat; add bread cubes and water; toss gently. Mix well and set aside.
2. Make a lengthwise cut about 1 1/2 inches deep along the center of each tenderloin.
- Spoon half of the mushroom mixture into the cut section.
- Pack firmly with kitchen string.
- Tie each loin securely in several places to hold the cut edges of meat.
3. Preheat broiler.
- Place both tenderloins, cut side up on a rack in a broiling pan.
- Broil 15 minutes. If necessary, cover stuffing with foil to prevent browning and drying out.
- Carefully turn tenderloins, cut side down, and continue broiling for another 15 minutes or until desired doneness.
4. Meanwhile, prepare the Brandied Tomato Gravy:
- Drain tomatoes; finely chop the remaining tomatoes.
- In a quart saucepan over medium heat, melt butter.
- Stir in flour until blended; cook 1 minute.
- Gradually stir in beef broth, reserved tomato liquid, reserved chopped tomatoes, brandy, pepper, meat extract paste, and salt. Cook, stirring constantly, until slightly thickened.
5. To serve, place loins on a cutting board. Let stand 10 minutes for easier slicing.
- Remove string, slice the meat.
- Arrange slices on a warm platter; garnish with watercress and cherry tomatoes.
- Yields about 10 servings.
Brandied Tomato Gravy:
- This flavorful gravy pairs perfectly with the broiled beef tenderloin, adding depth to the dish. Serve it alongside fresh green salad for a complete meal.
